mission.title: open-webui: feat: allow login without authentication
mission.description: feat: allow login without authentication. Requested by 47+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-webui deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-webui version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
helm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-webui installation.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
Many self hosted programs have an authentication-by-default approach these days.

steps.title: Apply the fix for feat: allow login without authentication
steps.description:
> I think there are probably still quite a few people that want no authentication all together but this is a good step forward.

Not gonna lie, I think I like this solution _better_ than building auth in ourselves, and it's been our stated opinion that we feel auth shouldn't be optional.
```yaml
services:
  open-webui:
    ...
    environment:
      WEBUI_AUTH_TRUSTED_EMAIL_HEADER: 'X-authentik-email'
    networks:
      - traefik-servicenet
    labels:
      traefik.enable: true
      traefik.http.routers.open-webui.middlewares: authentik
      traefik.http.middlewares.open-webui-auth.headers.customrequestheaders.X-authentik-email: "true"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "feat: allow login without authentication" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
> I think there are probably still quite a few people that want no authentication all together but this is a good step forward.

Not gonna lie, I think I like this solution _better_ than building auth in ourselves, and it's been our stated opinion that we feel auth shouldn't be optional.
codeSnippets:
services:
  open-webui:
    ...
    environment:
      WEBUI_AUTH_TRUSTED_EMAIL_HEADER: 'X-authentik-email'
    networks:
      - traefik-servicenet
    labels:
      traefik.enable: true
      traefik.http.routers.open-webui.middlewares: authentik
      traefik.http.middlewares.open-webui-auth.headers.customrequestheaders.X-authentik-email: "true"

&name open-webui:
    container_name: *name
    hostname: *name
    build:
      context: https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui.git#main
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    volumes:
      - /mnt/llm/open-webui/data:/app/backend/data
    depends_on:
      ollama:
        condition: service_started
        restart: false
    links:
      - ollama
    environment:
      OLLAMA_BASE_URL: http://ollama:11434
      SCARF_NO_ANALYTICS: true
      ANONYMIZED_TELEMETRY: false
      DO_NOT_TRACK: true
      ENABLE_SIGNUP: false
      WEBUI_AUTH: false
      RAG_EMBEDDING_MODEL_AUTO_UPDATE: true
      RAG_EMBEDDING_MODEL: "mixedbread-ai/mxbai-embed-large-v1"
    networks:
      - traefik-servicenet
      - default
    labels:
      traefik.enable: true
      traefik.http.routers.open-webui.rule: "Hos

docker stop open-webui
# (cleared all browser cookies/cache)

rm -rf /mnt/llm/open-webui
docker compose up -d open-webui

v0.1.124 - building the best open-source AI user interface.
https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui
